narrative.headline: 2022 Kia Sportage: the engine draws the most owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 42 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is by far the most reported area at 45%, well ahead of the electrical system and forward collision avoidance (12% each). Two engine reports involve a fire, and the electrical system and forward collision avoidance areas are each tied to 2 crashes. No injuries or deaths are reported.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint: 19 reports (45%), including 2 with fire. No crashes or injuries reported, but the fire count makes this worth a careful look.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 5 reports (12%), tied to 2 crashes. No injuries reported in this area.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 5 reports (12%), tied to 2 crashes. Owners describe the collision warning not activating when expected.

issues.title: Trailer Hitches
issues.blurb: 4 reports (10%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Several reference a trailer hitch recall.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The smallest area here: 3 reports (7%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe sudden loss of power while driving.
